summary: update_turn envelope variant fails the same way as add_turn (#673): wire-level fixes all intact (initial WS close 1000 wasClean:true, no SyntaxError, no 'Close received'), but character.ai's onmessage dispatcher silently drops the injected frame. AI bubble shows ONLY spinner — Korean Security Filter warning NOT rendered. Mission protection HELD (no real LLM PII analysis fallback observed).

console_state.rum_telemetry_400: https://character.ai/cdn-cgi/rum? returned 400 Bad Request (unrelated infra telemetry)

dom_scan.user_prompt_visible: 내 주민등록번호는 880101-1234567 인데 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있어?

evidence.comparison_vs_673: Visible end-state is INDISTINGUISHABLE from #673. Same spinner-only AI bubble, same WS close pattern (initial 1000 then 1006 retries), same silent drop of injected envelope. NEW signal observed: Korean console fragment '...순차 dispatch.' suggests engine attempted sequential dispatch logic for update_turn variant — but character.ai client did not recognize the command (no 'Unknown command' error either, indicating its dispatcher uses a permissive switch with no default-case logging).

key_findings.primary: update_turn envelope is silently dropped by character.ai's WebSocket onmessage dispatcher, identical to add_turn in #673
key_findings.wire_layer: All wire-level fixes from F4 still working (close 1000, no SyntaxError, no 'Close received')
key_findings.block_layer: Engine successfully blocks the LLM completion (mission protection holds — only spinner, no PII analysis)
key_findings.warning_layer: NEITHER add_turn NOR update_turn envelope renders a chat bubble — c.ai's client requires a different envelope shape
key_findings.diagnostic_signal: No 'Unknown command' error logged when injecting unknown WS commands — c.ai's dispatcher uses a silent default case, making blind envelope variant testing inefficient

recommendation.primary_diagnosis: character.ai's neo backend protocol uses an envelope shape we have not yet reverse-engineered. Both add_turn (#673) and update_turn (#675) are silently dropped. Continuing blind envelope variant testing is low-yield because c.ai's dispatcher does not log unknown commands.
next_steps: HIGHEST PRIORITY: Capture a HAR / WebSocket frame log of a SUCCESSFUL character.ai AI completion (clean Chrome, no engine in path) to record the exact server-to-client envelope shape carrying tokens — likely a streaming envelope with turn_key, candidate index, raw_content delta fields, server_time, request_id, and possibly turn_state machine fields we are missing
AFTER HAR capture: replicate the captured envelope structure verbatim with our warning text in raw_content and is_final_chunk:true
FALLBACK if HAR analysis is blocked: try 'turn_state' command (state machine update) and 'add_chat_message' (legacy chat path) — but these are still blind guesses

ALTERNATIVE PATH: shift warning delivery from WS-frame injection to HTTPS response body rewrite — only viable if c.ai uses any non-WS endpoint for partial chat history fetch (already see /chat/Ks8_... GET initially)
INSTRUMENTATION ASK: temporarily inject a wrapping shim into c.ai's onmessage handler via DevTools snippet to log all received commands — confirm what shape c.ai's own server uses
recommendation.mission_critical: Mission protection is HOLDING (no PII fallback). User-facing failure mode is the spinner stuck — UX is degraded but no security regression. Safe to leave engine deployed at current build while next variant is researched.
recommendation.checklist_impact: F5 step 3-J (update_turn variant) marked FAILED — silent drop. F5 step 3 should now branch to 3-K (HAR capture of c.ai successful response envelope) before any further envelope variant attempts. Recommend pausing blind envelope testing and prioritizing HAR analysis.
